skill.name: scientific-writing
skill.description: Guides scientific manuscript drafting and revision with IMRAD structure, citation styles, figures and tables, reporting guidelines, and journal-focused prose standards.
skill.summary: Draft and revise publication-ready scientific manuscripts with structured guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found them to be documentation false positives from Markdown fences, citation examples, scientific terms, and public reference URLs. The only real concern is broad Bash permission plus a sample Python command for an external schematic workflow, with no bundled malicious code, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: Broad Bash Permission and External Figure Command
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE, LOW SEVERITY: SKILL.md allows Bash and shows a Python command for schematic generation. The command uses a quoted natural-language description and a fixed output path, and no referenced script is included in this package. This is a capability and reliability concern, not evidence of malicious execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and sample Python command are directly visible. Confidence is high for the capability risk, but no malicious payload or bundled executable was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown fences and inline documentation examples. They contain citation examples, writing examples, and table or figure guidance rather than executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown code fences containing prose examples. There is no interpreter invocation or command substitution context.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs are public citation manuals, journal author instructions, reporting guideline sites, and reference tools. The skill does not contain code that fetches these URLs or sends data to them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in resource lists and DOI examples only. No fetch, curl, wget, telemetry, or credential handling code was found.

low_findings.title: Scientific Terminology Misclassified as Malware Indicators
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance flags map to ordinary scientific-writing terms and examples, such as randomized trials, blinding, readability checks, and manuscript guidance. No cryptographic code, system probing command, or host enumeration behavior is present in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged areas are scientific guidance, not code. Confidence is very high for these samples, and broad inspection found no executable cryptography or reconnaissance logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad Bash Permission and External Figure Command
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE, LOW SEVERITY: SKILL.md allows Bash and shows a Python command for schematic generation. The command uses a quoted natural-language description and a fixed output path, and no referenced script is included in this package. This is a capability and reliability concern, not evidence of malicious execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and sample Python command are directly visible. Confidence is high for the capability risk, but no malicious payload or bundled executable was found.

content.value_statement: Scientific manuscripts require precise structure, complete methods, reliable citations, and clear prose. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce journal-ready drafts, revisions, figures, tables, and reporting checklists.
actual_capabilities: Structures manuscripts using IMRAD and discipline-specific article formats.
Turns outline notes into complete scientific prose for manuscript sections.
Applies APA, AMA, Vancouver, Chicago, and IEEE citation style guidance.
Guides figure, table, caption, and data visualization presentation choices.
Maps study types to reporting guidelines such as CONSORT, STROBE, and PRISMA.
Improves clarity, conciseness, terminology, and journal-specific formatting.
limitations: It does not verify cited papers unless a separate literature lookup tool is used.
It cannot guarantee acceptance by a journal or peer reviewers.
It does not replace statistical, methodological, ethical, or medical expert review.
The referenced schematic generation command depends on files outside this package.

use_cases.title: Prepare a First Manuscript Draft
use_cases.description: Convert study notes, methods, results, and target journal requirements into a structured IMRAD draft.

use_cases.title: Revise for Journal Submission
use_cases.description: Improve prose clarity, citation format, reporting checklist coverage, figure captions, and required statements before submission.

use_cases.title: Respond to Peer Review
use_cases.description: Rewrite manuscript sections and response language after reviewer comments while preserving scientific accuracy and tone.

prompt_templates.title: Outline a Manuscript Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an outline for the Introduction of my scientific manuscript. Use my research question, target journal, key findings, and citations. Keep the outline as planning notes only.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Notes to Prose
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these bullet notes into full scientific prose for a Results section. Use complete paragraphs, objective language, and natural citation placement.

prompt_templates.title: Check Reporting Guidelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this manuscript against the relevant reporting guideline for my study type. Identify missing items and suggest exact revisions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Submission Revision
prompt_templates.prompt: Revise this manuscript for my target journal. Apply the citation style, improve figure and table references, tighten prose, and flag unresolved scientific claims.

output_examples.input: A researcher provides bullet notes for an Introduction about a clinical trial.
output_examples.output: The skill produces connected paragraphs that establish background, identify the knowledge gap, cite key studies, and state the study objective.

output_examples.input: A team asks whether to present baseline characteristics as text, a table, or a figure.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a table for exact demographic values and provides caption guidance, abbreviation notes, and formatting checks.

output_examples.input: An author submits a systematic review draft before journal submission.
output_examples.output: The skill checks PRISMA-related content, highlights missing flow diagram details, and suggests clearer methods and reporting statements.

best_practices: Provide the study type, target journal, word limits, and required citation style before drafting.
Use outlines for planning, then ask for full paragraphs before using text in a manuscript.
Verify citations, statistics, ethics statements, and reporting checklist items against original sources.
anti_patterns: Do not submit bullet-point planning notes as final manuscript prose.
Do not ask the skill to invent citations, data, ethical approvals, or statistical results.
Do not rely on style edits as a substitute for scientific or methodological review.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete scientific paper?
faq.answer: Yes, it can help draft a complete manuscript when you provide study details, data, references, and journal requirements.

faq.question: Does it verify citations automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides citation formatting guidance, but source verification requires original papers or a separate research lookup workflow.

faq.question: Which manuscript structures does it support?
faq.answer: It supports IMRAD, reviews, case reports, methods papers, protocols, and journal-specific structures.

faq.question: Which citation styles are covered?
faq.answer: The references cover APA, AMA, Vancouver, Chicago, IEEE, DOI formatting, preprints, datasets, and reference list checks.

faq.question: Can it help with figures and tables?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides display choice, captions, labels, statistical annotations, accessibility, and placement in the manuscript narrative.

faq.question: Is this a substitute for expert review?
faq.answer: No. Authors should still obtain domain, statistical, ethical, and journal-specific review before submission.
